channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan Kwame Otiende Elected Councillor for London Fields in Historic Win
item.description: Kwame Otiende, a Kenyan from Homa Bay County, has made history by winning a seat at the Hackney Council, representing London Fields on the Green Party ticket. He won alongside Anntoinette Bramble and ...

item.title: Sindh government schedules May salaries and pensions ahead of Eid
item.description: KARACHI: The Sindh government has announced that salaries and pensions for the month of May will be released ahead of Eid ul Adha, providing timely financial relief to public servants. According to an...

item.title: One Nation Claims Historic Victory in Farrer By-election, Ending Longstanding Liberal Hold
item.description: One Nation has achieved a significant victory in the Farrer by-election, unseating the Liberal Party in a seat it had maintained for almost 80 years. The result represents a major shift in the federal...

item.title: Vijay to Take Oath as Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu Tomorrow
item.description: According to reports circulating online and from reliable political sources, popular actor-turned-politician Vijay is officially set to become the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu. The swearing-in ceremon...

item.title: US alliances face growing strain as Trump’s Iran war decisions unsettle partners
item.description: This report explains how President Donald Trump’s recent decisions during the Iran conflict are causing serious tension between the United States and its traditional allies across Europe, the Middle E...

item.title: BUK students get more time to apply for NELFUND loans for 2025/2026 session
item.description: Bayero University, Kano (BUK) don announce say dem don extend the Nigerian Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) application period for students wey dey eligible for 2025/2026 academic session. According to s...

item.title: Reflections on Praying to the Holy Spirit
item.description: In this article, Fr. Raymund Benedict 'RB' Hizon, SJ, shares insights on the spiritual practice of praying to the Holy Spirit. He emphasizes the importance of seeking guidance, wisdom, and comfort fro...

item.title: Victor Osimhen scores twice as Galatasaray clinch fourth consecutive Turkish League title
item.description: Victor Osimhen, Super Eagles striker, was the main man as Galatasaray lifted the Turkish League title for the fourth time in a row. The match against Antalyaspor in Istanbul ended 4-2, with Osimhen sc...

item.title: Hantavirus Cases in South Africa Linked to Tourists, Not Local Spread
item.description: South Africa has reported two cases of hantavirus, both involving international tourists who were on a cruise from Southern Argentina to the Canary Islands. One tourist collapsed at OR Tambo Internati...

item.title: Starmer outlines Labour’s strategy following significant local election losses
item.description: Following a significant setback in the local elections, where Labour lost over 1,200 council seats in England and control of 37 councils, Sir Keir Starmer has emphasised that addressing the consequenc...

item.title: Six Passengers Killed in Mandera East Matatu Attack
item.description: On Saturday, May 9, 2026, a tragic attack occurred in Mandera East when six passengers in a 14-seater matatu were killed and several others injured. The vehicle was heading from Mandera town to Arabia...

item.title: Canvas Cyberattack Affects Canadian Universities and Global Schools
item.description: A major cybersecurity incident has impacted thousands of educational institutions worldwide, including several leading Canadian universities such as the University of Toronto, University of British Co...

item.title: Opening Ceremonies for 2026 FIFA World Cup to Feature Global Music Stars
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is set to start with exciting celebrations blending football and music. African artists Tyla and Rema will join a global lineup of performers at the tournament's opening events...

item.title: Police arrest suspect over killing of couple in Kebbi village
item.description: Police for Kebbi State don arrest one 25-year-old man, Idrisu Musa, wey dem accuse say e attack and kill one couple for Asarara village, inside Kimba District for Jega Local Government Area. According...

item.title: Iran Warns US Amid Escalating Tensions as Ceasefire Shows Limited Impact
item.description: Tensions between Iran and the United States remain high as Iran warned of a 'heavy assault' against U.S. assets in the Middle East if Iranian ships are attacked during the current ceasefire. The recen...

item.title: National poll shows broad bipartisan concern about money’s role in U.S. politics
item.description: A new POLITICO Poll finds that most Americans believe there is too much money in politics, a view that crosses party lines as midterm campaign spending approaches record levels. The survey shows that ...

item.title: Canberra man struggles with unpaid fines after buyer fails to transfer car registration
item.description: Tom Stevens, a tow-truck driver from Isabella Plains, Canberra, has been dealing with traffic fines linked to a car he sold almost three years ago. In July 2023, he sold his 1999 Toyota Camry on Faceb...

item.title: BJP Government's First Oath Ceremony in West Bengal Marked by Cultural Symbolism
item.description: In a historic moment for West Bengal, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) formed its first government in the state, marked by significant cultural and political symbolism. Prime Minister Narendra Modi to...

item.title: UK Prepares Warship for Strait of Hormuz Security Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Britain has sent its air defence destroyer, HMS Dragon, to the Middle East in preparation for a potential multinational mission to protect shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The deployment follows...

item.title: Leinster pull away late to beat Lions 31-7 in URC clash
item.description: The Lions put in a gutsy performance against defending URC champions Leinster in Dublin, staying competitive for most of the match before fading in the final stages to lose 31-7. For around 70 minutes...
